name: ADRENOCORTICAL HYPERPLASIA
definition: ADRENAL CORTICAL HYPERPLASIA MACRONODULAR HYPERPLASIA WITH MARKED ADRENAL ENLARGEMENT
dominant_finding: bilateral enlarged adrenal glands (often > 5 cm) with macronodules
discriminating_features: smooth to slightly lobular thickening of entire adrenal gland while maintaining its
focal hypo- to isoattenuating micro- / macronodules (= nodules up to 2.5 cm)
background of normal adrenal tissue / atrophic intervening cortex
asymmetric bilateral NP-59 uptake (related to urinary cortisol excretion) WITHOUT
bilateral foci of NP-59 uptake WITH dexamethasone suppression (nondiagnostic ≥ 5 days)

general: smooth to slightly lobular thickening of entire adrenal gland while maintaining its
focal hypo- to isoattenuating micro- / macronodules (= nodules up to 2.5 cm)
background of normal adrenal tissue / atrophic intervening cortex
ANGIO: normal venogram / may show enlarged gland
adrenal venous sampling to direct therapy in symptomatic patients ← results NOT ALWAYS
NUC: asymmetric bilateral NP-59 uptake (related to urinary cortisol excretion) WITHOUT
bilateral foci of NP-59 uptake WITH dexamethasone suppression (nondiagnostic ≥ 5 days)
bilateral enlarged adrenal glands (often > 5 cm) with macronodules
normal to slightly enlarged adrenals + small nodules + atrophy of intervening cortex

clinical.Rx: no treatment in the absence of clinical / biochemical evidence of adrenocortical
